name: B-59 Conning Tower
description: The nerve center of the submarine — a cramped, vertical cylinder of sweating steel barely wide enough for three men to stand without touching, lit by a single red emergency lamp bolted above the chart table. The air here is the worst on the boat: the heat from the ballast control panels and the press of bodies has pushed the temperature past 105°F, and every surface is slick with condensation.
atmosphericDetails: The chart table is bolted to the starboard bulkhead at chest height; Savitsky's parallel rulers and dividers are laid out with the precision of a man who needs order around him.
A brass key receptacle is mounted directly above the chart table — one of three distributed across the boat — its keyhole dark and waiting.
Condensation drips from the overhead pipe bundle at irregular intervals, landing on the chart paper with a sound like a slow clock.
